migration.semantics: All entries render through the real-component registry; draft entries remain unpromoted until research, authored anatomy, accessibility, visual QA, and review gates pass.

categories.name: Actions & commands
categories.scope: Invoke, authorize, or reverse an operation.

categories.name: Inputs & selection
categories.scope: Enter, edit, choose, or refine a value.

categories.name: Navigation & wayfinding
categories.scope: Move between or orient within places, views, or steps.

categories.name: Content & data
categories.scope: Inspect, compare, organize, or work with information.

categories.name: Feedback & status
categories.scope: Understand progress, outcome, validation, or recovery.

categories.name: Surfaces & disclosure
categories.scope: Reveal temporary, contextual, or conditional content or controls.

categories.name: Layout & composition
categories.scope: Arrange persistent regions, panes, or a workspace.

taxonomy.categoryTieBreakRule: Classify by the user’s primary goal in the record definition. If roles remain tied, choose the role that distinguishes the record from its see-also neighbors; structural form belongs in kind.

sources.name: HTML and ARIA in HTML
sources.reuse: Paraphrase. Prefer native HTML; ARIA must not contradict it.

sources.name: WCAG 2.2
sources.reuse: Paraphrase; retain required W3C notices and source context if material is reproduced.

sources.name: WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
sources.role: Keyboard, focus, role, and state conventions for custom widgets.
sources.status: Informative guidance, not a design system.
sources.reuse: Paraphrase and validate browser and assistive-technology support.

sources.name: Cognitive Accessibility Guidance
sources.role: Review lens for forms, navigation, errors, memory, timing, and hidden content.
sources.status: Supplemental guidance; it does not change WCAG conformance.
sources.reuse: Use as an additional review lens.

sources.name: Design Tokens Format Module 2025.10
sources.role: Foundational vocabulary for tokens, values, groups, and aliases.
sources.status: Version-pinned interchange specification, not a component taxonomy.
sources.reuse: Use terminology and confirm the exact license before reproducing diagrams or examples.

sources.name: Open UI
sources.role: Cross-system component anatomy, state, and naming research.
sources.status: Mature terms can inform names; drafts are evidence, not rules.
sources.reuse: Confirm the specific page or repository license before reuse.

sources.name: GOV.UK Design System
sources.status: Reference system; it does not set the Atlas visual style.
sources.reuse: Attribute reused material under OGL v3.0 and exclude marks or third-party material.

sources.name: Human-AI interaction guidance
sources.role: Research-grounded guidance for expectations, control, correction, feedback, failure, and behavior over time.
sources.status: Specialized human-AI guidance; it informs interaction boundaries rather than defining web semantics.
sources.reuse: Paraphrase findings and guidance. Respect the PAIR guidebook license; do not reuse vendor visuals.

sources.name: Generative AI pattern systems
sources.role: Current vocabulary for AI labels, context, progress, approvals, artifacts, agents, regeneration, and recovery.
sources.status: Emerging product-design patterns, not universal standards.
sources.reuse: Use for terminology and coverage checks. Draw original neutral examples; do not copy code, assets, or visual language.

sources.name: SaaS and enterprise design systems
sources.role: Coverage checks for collaboration, data density, onboarding, object handling, workflows, administration, and governance.
sources.status: Product-specific systems; terms are neutralized before entering the Atlas.
sources.reuse: Paraphrase and use for coverage checks only. Do not reproduce branded assets, screenshots, or styling.

reviewEvidence.method: Canonical source register plus per-entry audit reference review
Official Computer Use capture and editorial assessment of 107 gallery and 107 inspector views
Official Computer Use review of the real-component gallery from first through final rows
Deterministic semantic DOM and Atlas DS accessibility contract tests
Strict TypeScript, immutable-ID coverage, dependency, and promotion-gate validation
Official Computer Use inspection of the revised identity and governance anatomy
Official Computer Use inspection of stable destination context and scoped loading status

catalog.name: Button
catalog.description: Triggers a discrete action from a clear, labeled control.
catalog.details: Use a concise action label. Icon-only and floating variants remain buttons when they perform the same primary job.

research.question: What establishes “Button” as project-agnostic UI vocabulary, and what behavior boundaries distinguish it?
research.conclusion: Triggers a discrete action from a clear, labeled control. Use a concise action label. Icon-only and floating variants remain buttons when they perform the same primary job.

evidence.claim: Prefer native HTML names and semantics where the platform defines the control.
Treat WCAG 2.2 as the testable accessibility floor.

card.title: Button
card.description: Triggers a discrete action from a clear, labeled control.

catalog.name: Toggle button
catalog.description: A button that stays pressed or unpressed to show whether an action mode is active.
catalog.details: Use for a persistent action state such as mute or bold. Use Switch for an on/off setting and Checkbox for selecting an option.

research.question: What establishes “Toggle button” as project-agnostic UI vocabulary, and what behavior boundaries distinguish it?
research.conclusion: A button that stays pressed or unpressed to show whether an action mode is active. Use for a persistent action state such as mute or bold. Use Switch for an on/off setting and Checkbox for selecting an option.

evidence.claim: Check keyboard, focus, role, state, and property conventions for custom widgets.
Compare cross-system anatomy and naming while treating drafts as evidence rather than rules.

card.title: Toggle button
card.description: A button that stays pressed or unpressed to show whether an action mode is active.

catalog.name: Link
catalog.description: Moves someone to another place, resource, or view.
catalog.details: Use a Link for navigation and a Button for an action that changes state, submits work, or opens a process.

research.question: What establishes “Link” as project-agnostic UI vocabulary, and what behavior boundaries distinguish it?
research.conclusion: Moves someone to another place, resource, or view. Use a Link for navigation and a Button for an action that changes state, submits work, or opens a process.

evidence.claim: Prefer native HTML names and semantics where the platform defines the control.
Treat WCAG 2.2 as the testable accessibility floor.

card.title: Link
